title: Mass shooting in Austin investigated as potential terror act | 7NEWS
description: A mass shooting in downtown Austin, Texas has left 14 people injured after a 53-year-old naturalised citizen from Senegal opened fire in a bar. The suspect was reportedly wearing a shirt coloured in the Iranian flag with the slogan 'Property of Allah'. Authorities are investigating the incident as a potential act of terror, though no confirmed link to Middle East conflicts has been established. title: Bullets fired into home in western Sydney
description: Police have responded to reports of gunfire at a home in Lakemba in Sydney's west, finding spent bullet casings and damage to the property. #police #crime #investigation #lakemba #westernsydney

title: E-bike rider uses trolley to tear up soccer field
description: A teenage e-bike rider has been captured dragging a shopping trolley across a sports complex in the Gold Coast, flinging it at goal nets and light posts. #ebike #escooter #soccer #field #goldcoast

title: Powerful dust storm hits Western Australia
description: A large dust storm has coated Western Australia's Pilbara region red after 110km/h winds pushed a blanket of dust across the area. #duststorm #sandstorm #pilbara #westernaustralia #storm

title: Fighter jet falls from sky over Kuwait
description: Three US fighter jets have crashed over Kuwait in a suspected incident of friendly fire.  US Central Command confirmed the three F-15E Strike Eagles were actively fighting Iranian aircraft, ballistic missiles and drones when they were struck at 2pm AEST.  It’s believed the jets “were mistakenly shot down by Kuwaiti air defences”, Central Command said.  “Kuwait has acknowledged this incident and we are grateful for the efforts of the Kuwaiti defense forces and their support in this ongoing operation,” it said.  All six service members on the jets safely ejected and are currently in a stable condition. #planecrash #crash #fighterjet #iran #kuwait

title: ‘Walk of Shame’ installation spotted in Washington, DC
description: A Jeffrey Epstein-themed “Walk of Shame” installation has been set up in Farragut Square in Washington, DC. Video taken by AMK Recording shows stars modeled after the Hollywood Walk of Fame, naming public figures mentioned in publicly available Epstein-related documents.   Each star includes a QR code linking to articles or public records discussing the individual’s reported ties to Epstein. #WalkofShame #WalkofFame #WashingtonDC #worldnews

title: Hillary Clinton storms out of Epstein deposition | 7NEWS
description: Hillary Clinton abruptly ended her deposition regarding Jeffrey Epstein after a photograph from her closed-door testimony was leaked to the media. The former Secretary of State threatened to walk out permanently and told lawmakers they could hold her in contempt, expressing frustration over what she characterised as typical behaviour. Clinton maintains she has no recollection of ever meeting Jeffrey Epstein. title: Stolen car splits in half, driver vanishes | 7NEWS
description: A stolen Toyota Kluger crashed into a tree in Seabrook, Melbourne, at approximately 1am, splitting the vehicle completely in half. The car had been taken just five minutes earlier during a carjacking at Laverton Railway Station. Despite an extensive search by police and the dog squad, the driver remains missing, with authorities holding grave concerns due to the severity of injuries likely sustained in the crash. title: Iran war sparks fuel price fears as Aussie motorists rush to fill up
description: Australians are rushing to fill up their cars amid fears war in the Middle East will send petrol prices soaring. Some service stations have already run out of fuel overnight and are now rushing to replenish supplies, with just over a month of petrol and diesel remaining in reserve. #fuel #petrol #diesel #iran #middleeast

title: Murder charge after deadly Mount Barker confrontation | 7NEWS
description: A 25-year-old man from Paraka has been charged with murder following a fatal confrontation on Paterson Lane in Mount Barker, South Australia. The 39-year-old victim was dragged into a Holden Captiva and driven to hospital by a group of approximately seven people after collapsing on nearby Muldoon Street, but died shortly after arrival. Major crime detectives are investigating the incident, which police say involved two men known to each other and was not a random attack. title: Power switched on for Western Sydney Airport | 7NEWS
description: Western Sydney Airport's new transmission substation has been switched on as part of a $320 million power project for the Aerotropolis area. The energy infrastructure is designed to service an additional 1.5 million people expected to move to Western Sydney over the next decade and support up to 200,000 new jobs. The airport is scheduled to open in October with capacity for 10 million passengers annually, alongside the planned city of Bradfield spanning 11,000 hectares. title: Gender pay gap narrows as Victoria mandates work-from-home rights | 7NEWS
description: New research from the Workplace Gender Equality Agency reveals the gender pay gap in Australia has narrowed by just under 1%, with the biggest improvements occurring in lower-income sectors where women are overrepresented. However, significant inequality persists in high-paying industries like banking and airlines, where men dominate senior leadership and executive positions. The Victorian government has announced plans to enshrine the right to work from home two days a week, including for small businesses, following a survey of 37,000 respondents showing 75% consider remote work extremely important. title: Armed man shot dead by police in Potts Point, Sydney
description: A man has been shot dead by police during an alleged assault on two women at Potts Point in Sydney's inner-east. #pottspoint #crime #sydneycrime #sydney #nswpolice

title: US strikes on Iran breach international law | 7NEWS
description: US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran have raised serious concerns among international law experts about violations of fundamental post-World War II prohibitions on the use of force. Dr Tamer Morris from Sydney University explains that the attacks lack legal justification under international law, as Iran posed no imminent threat and the UN Security Council was bypassed. The breach of these fundamental norms threatens global stability and leaves middle-power nations like Australia vulnerable, as countries can no longer rely on international law for protection and may instead turn to military build-up and nuclear weapons programs.
